meta.name: The Unlikely Mayor Tour
meta.description: Ten towns. Ten elections. One increasingly tired candidate. Win each election to unlock the next stop on your improbable rise to power.

scenarios.title: Mom Said Run
scenarios.subtitle: HOMETOWN
scenarios.opponent_tagline: 80 years old. Has been mayor since before you were born. Has forgotten he is running. Has possibly forgotten he is mayor.

intro_lines.speaker: Mom
intro_lines.text: Sweetie, I signed you up for the mayor election. You weren't doing anything anyway.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: Mom, I have a LOT going on right now.

intro_lines.speaker: Mom
intro_lines.text: You microwaved cereal yesterday.

intro_lines.speaker: Mom
intro_lines.text: Anyway, the deposit's non-refundable. Go beat Earl. He's been mayor for 52 years and the pothole is still there.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: ...Fine. But only because of the pothole.

scenarios.epilogue_win: Mom puts your victory in the church newsletter. The pothole remains unfixed, but it's YOUR pothole now. Word of your win travels. A college town calls...
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Earl wins again, mostly by accident. Mom says she's 'not disappointed, just recalibrating.' Try again, sweetie.

scenarios.title: Campus of Chaos
scenarios.subtitle: COLLEGE TOWN
scenarios.main_crisis: The Protest About the Last Protest
scenarios.opponent_tagline: Postmodern Philosophy professor. Speaks exclusively in metaphors. Nobody has understood a single position he holds, which polls extremely well.

intro_lines.speaker: Student Organizer
intro_lines.text: Welcome to Wexford! Quick heads up: we're protesting the administration, the weather, and one specific vending machine.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: What did the vending machine do?

intro_lines.speaker: Student Organizer
intro_lines.text: It knows what it did.

intro_lines.speaker: Student Organizer
intro_lines.text: Anyway, you're running against Professor Verb. Last debate, he answered every question with 'but what IS a road?' and got a standing ovation.

scenarios.epilogue_win: The frat's unaccredited polling agency declares you the winner at 142%. Close enough. The suburbs have heard of you — and the HOAs are sharpening their clipboards.
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Professor Verb wins. His acceptance speech is one long metaphor about rivers. Students weep. You don't get it. Try again.

scenarios.title: HOA Wars
scenarios.subtitle: SUBURBAN TOWN
scenarios.opponent_tagline: Chairs all five HOAs simultaneously, which should be illegal and technically is. Campaigns door-to-door with a clipboard and a smile that has ended marriages.

intro_lines.speaker: Brenda
intro_lines.text: Welcome to Pleasant Verge! I see you've parked... like that.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: I've been here forty seconds.

intro_lines.speaker: Brenda
intro_lines.text: And already in violation of bylaws 7, 12, and — oh dear — 44(b). Welcome to the race, sweetheart. I do hope your campaign signs are regulation beige.

scenarios.epilogue_win: You win. Brenda demands a recount, an inspection of your gutters, and your immediate emotional collapse. She receives none. A rustbelt town wants to meet the HOA slayer.
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Brenda wins all five precincts and fines you for losing 'in a non-regulation manner.' Try again.

scenarios.title: Industry of Problems
scenarios.subtitle: RUSTBELT TOWN
scenarios.main_crisis: The Smoke That Is Sometimes Purple
scenarios.opponent_tagline: Former factory foreman. Beloved by everyone. Stubborn as the rust itself. Once fought the power plant and the power plant apologized.

intro_lines.speaker: Union Rep
intro_lines.text: Before you campaign here, you should know: everything's unionized. The diner. The pigeons. The vending machines.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: The vending machines have a union?

intro_lines.speaker: Union Rep
intro_lines.text: Local 7-Up. Don't cross them. Last guy who did got exact-changed to death.

intro_lines.speaker: Union Rep
intro_lines.text: You're up against Big Dan. Good man. Wrong about everything since 1987, but good man.

scenarios.epilogue_win: Big Dan shakes your hand so hard your watch stops. 'You'll do right by us,' he says. The purple smoke turns a respectful gray for one day. A resort town calls — they have tourist problems.
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Big Dan wins. The town celebrates with a parade that is also somehow a strike. Try again.

scenarios.title: Vacation Nightmare
scenarios.subtitle: TOURIST RETREAT
scenarios.opponent_tagline: Travel vlogger, 4.2M followers. Livestreams everything including this sentence. Campaign slogan: 'SMASH that vote button.'

intro_lines.speaker: Local
intro_lines.text: Thank goodness you're here. The tourists have gone feral again. One of them is living in the lighthouse. He pays no taxes. He answers to no law.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: Have you tried asking him to leave?

intro_lines.speaker: Local
intro_lines.text: He gave us a one-star review.

intro_lines.speaker: Skyler Jetlag
intro_lines.text: Heyyy what's up VoteFam, I'm here with my OPPONENT — say hi to the stream! Don't forget to like, subscribe, and REGISTER!

scenarios.epilogue_win: You win. Skyler's concession speech gets 12M views and a brand deal. The lighthouse man votes for you, illegally, twice. Silicon Gulch has noticed your numbers...
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Skyler wins and immediately leaves for a collab in Bali. The town is governed via vlog. Try again.

scenarios.title: Code, Drones & Drama
scenarios.subtitle: TECHNOLOGY TOWN
scenarios.opponent_tagline: 26-year-old CEO of the company that owns the town. Debates using AI deepfakes of himself, which are noticeably more charismatic.

intro_lines.speaker: Drone
intro_lines.text: WELCOME TO SILICON GULCH. YOUR APPROVAL RATING IS 12%. THIS MESSAGE WAS BROUGHT TO YOU BY YOUR OPPONENT.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: Did that drone just insult me?

intro_lines.speaker: Drone
intro_lines.text: INSULT MODULE: PREMIUM FEATURE. THAT WAS THE FREE TIER.

intro_lines.speaker: Blaze
intro_lines.text: Hey hey hey! Love the energy. Quick thing — the city's apps are down, democracy is in maintenance mode, and I've pivoted the fire department to a subscription model. You're gonna love it here.

scenarios.epilogue_win: You win. The parking app concedes on Blaze's behalf, then demands a pardon. The fire department is freed from its paywall. Then you fall asleep at your desk, exhausted...
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Blaze wins via a get-out-the-vote push notification with a 'VOTE NOW (1 free trial)' button. Try again.

scenarios.title: Welcome to Naptropolis
scenarios.subtitle: DREAM SEQUENCE
scenarios.opponent_tagline: Your own subconscious, with maxed-out stats and your exact face but with better posture. Knows every mistake you've ever made. Brings them up casually.

intro_lines.text: Welcome to Naptropolis. Population: your doubts.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: This is a dream, isn't it. The trees are pink. The fountain is whispering poll numbers.

intro_lines.speaker: You, But Better
intro_lines.text: Hello. I'm you, if you'd stretched. I'll be running against you today, using all the lines you thought of two hours too late.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: I want to wake up.

intro_lines.speaker: You, But Better
intro_lines.text: And yet you registered to run. Interesting.

scenarios.epilogue_win: You beat yourself. Therapists everywhere feel a disturbance. You wake up on your desk in Silicon Gulch, drooling on a ballot, finally sure of one thing: you actually want this. Next stop: the big leagues.
scenarios.epilogue_lose: You lose to yourself, which is somehow worse than every previous loss combined. You wake up anyway. The doubt remains. Re-enter the dream and finish it.

scenarios.title: Mayhem District
scenarios.subtitle: CRIME CITY
scenarios.opponent_tagline: Ex-lawyer with a suspiciously spotless record. So clean it loops back around to filthy. Sirens play whenever he makes a campaign promise, which he insists is 'a coincidence.'

intro_lines.speaker: Cab Driver
intro_lines.text: East Mayhem, huh? Word of advice: the Dockside crew runs the east, the Council of Aunts runs the west, and downtown belongs to the raccoons.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: And elections here are... legal?

intro_lines.speaker: Cab Driver
intro_lines.text: Extremely legal. Confusingly legal. So legal it makes you nervous, you know?

intro_lines.speaker: Sal
intro_lines.text: A new candidate! Wonderful. I'd shake your hand but mine are famously clean. Ask anyone. Ask them twice if the first answer seems scared.

scenarios.epilogue_win: You win. The Council of Aunts approves. The raccoons send a tribute of shiny objects. Sal congratulates you with a smile that's already planning something. National media arrives in town...
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Sal wins with 99.8% turnout, including several cemeteries. 'Coincidence,' he says, as sirens play. Try again.

scenarios.title: Trial of the Titans
scenarios.subtitle: NATIONAL TEST TOWN
scenarios.opponent_tagline: TV personality who transitioned into politics yesterday, around 4 PM. Already has three biographies and a scandal documentary in pre-production.

intro_lines.speaker: Reporter
intro_lines.text: We're LIVE in Bellwether Falls, the town that predicts every election! With us: the candidate who beat an HOA, a vlogger, and reportedly THEMSELVES?

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: The dream one is hard to explain.

intro_lines.speaker: Reporter
intro_lines.text: And your opponent, Tawny Spectacle, who announced her candidacy during a commercial break for her own show!

intro_lines.speaker: Tawny
intro_lines.text: Darling. When I lose — IF I lose — the documentary needs a villain. Smile for the cameras.

scenarios.epilogue_win: You win the town that predicts everything. Pundits declare you 'nationally viable' and 'probably a real person.' The Capital awaits. Every opponent you've ever beaten is watching.
scenarios.epilogue_lose: Tawny wins and announces it via a 6-part docuseries. You appear in episode 3 as 'The Setback.' Try again.

scenarios.title: The Final Mayor
scenarios.subtitle: CAPITAL CITY
scenarios.opponent_tagline: A procedurally optimized super-candidate, assembled by every machine, committee, and consultant you've ever annoyed. Built specifically to counter you. Has Earl's stamina, Brenda's clipboard, and Sal's hands.

intro_lines.speaker: Aide
intro_lines.text: Welcome to Bureaucratia. The government shuts down Thursday, the lobbyists have lobbyists, and the monument is protesting itself.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: I've seen worse. I ran a town where the ducks had right of way.

intro_lines.speaker: The Composite Candidate
intro_lines.text: HELLO. I AM EVERY OPPONENT YOU HAVE EVER FACED, FOCUS-GROUPED INTO ONE ELECTABLE SHAPE. YOUR MOTHER SAYS HELLO. SHE IS PROUD. WE POLLED HER.

intro_lines.speaker: You
intro_lines.text: ...Let's finish this.

scenarios.epilogue_win: You did it. From Mom's kitchen to the Capital. The Composite Candidate dissolves into a cloud of consultant invoices. Mom calls: 'I never doubted you. The deposit paid off.' Somewhere, a pothole in Dillard's Hollow is finally — no, it's still there. Some things are eternal. THE END.
scenarios.epilogue_lose: The Composite Candidate wins and is immediately stuck in committee, forever. The capital remains ungoverned, which nobody notices. You came so far. Finish it.
